mnemonics: No Advantage Shifting Here (NASH): Remember that in a Nash Equilibrium, no single player can gain an advantage by unilaterally changing their strategy.
keyPoints: A Nash Equilibrium is a set of strategies, one for each player, such that no player has incentive to change their strategy given what the others are doing.
It is a solution concept where each player's strategy is optimal against the strategies of the others.
A Nash Equilibrium does not necessarily lead to the best overall outcome for the group (it may be a Prisoner's Dilemma).
commonMistakes: Assuming a Nash Equilibrium always represents the best collective payoff (it can be Pareto inefficient).
Confusing a Nash Equilibrium with a dominant strategy equilibrium (all dominant strategy equilibria are Nash, but not all Nash equilibria are dominant).
